Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Requiring Vexatious Litigant Plaintiff To Furnish Security
Off Calendar Jun-24-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT PROGRESS FOUNDATION'S Motion For Order Requiring Vexatious Litigant Plaintiff To Furnish Security.

Appearances:

Janet Tapia, in court, in propria persona, Plaintiff.

Brittany Greenhill, Esq., of GORDON REES SCULLY MANSUKHANI, LLP, (415) 986-5900, for Defendant, via Zoom.

The matter is argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling. Progress Foundation's "motion for order requiring vexatious litigant plaintiff to furnish security" in the amount of $30,000 is granted. Pro per vexatious litigant Janet Tapia is notorious for taking advantage of the generous social services provided in San Francisco, then bringing meritless suits against the service providers, costing them tens of thousands in attorney fees.

Tapia filed no opposition to Progress Foundation's motion, and instead filed a "motion for order waiving of posting a bond" for an uncertain hearing date (July 5 or July 24). Tapia's motion says she lacks the means to furnish security of $30,000, but "the vexatious litigant statutes owhere require the trial court to take into consideration the plaintiff's means in determining the amount of security to be required." (Devereaux v. Latham & Watkins (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 1571, 1588.) Instead, the statutes focus on the harm threatened by vexatious litigants- and $30,000 is a reasonable estimate here.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: R. Woods. Reported by For The Record (FTR). (302/RBU)
